entry.title: Do you know the functionalities and concepts of SAP Field Service Management?
entry.content:
I  recommend to access our Implementing SAP Field Service Management learning journey.

Overview
This Learning Journey provides an in-depth understanding of the key business scenarios supported by SAP Field Service Management, while illustrating its main functionalities and concepts.
SAP Field Service Management supports dispatchers with real-time, automated workforce scheduling and planning, as well as providing service technicians with powerful apps to fulfill field service needs.

Learning objectives
After completing this intermediate level learning journey, Implementation Consultants will understand the full functionality of the web- and mobile applications offered by the SAP Field Service Management solution. They will understand standard product features, perform configurations and customizations using the Field Service Management Administration Module and they will know use case scenarios such as administrator, dispatcher, and field technician.
Furthermore they will be able to provide first level support and they know where to get additional information, best practices and documentation.

Prerequisites

  • Managing SAP Field Service Management

Pass all the quizzes and receive a digital badge

Please ask a question related to the digital learning Journey in the Q&A area. 

Our SAP Learning Experts will get back to you as soon as possible! We are here to support you. 
I appreciate your feedback and we will make sure to continue sharing interesting topics.

Kind regards
Margit

entry.title: Re: sap 3rd sales process flow
entry.content:
Hello @ARUPGHOSH 

Please find the standard Scope items for Third Party Sales

Sales Processing using Third-Party with Shipping Notification (BD3)

Sales Processing Using Third-Party Without Shipping Notification (BDK)

Best Regards

Vivek Savant

entry.title: Monitoring ASE database with SAP Cloud ALM
entry.content:
Dear,

How can I monitoring an ASE database with SAP Cloud ALM, please ?

Thanks in advance

entry.title: Re: Use of Account type option into Chart of account SAP B1
entry.content:
E stands for Expenses, I for Incoming, and N for Other.
Financial and income statements evaluate the type of account in the country's accounting plan to use them or place them in the appropriate places.

entry.title: Re: How to get the cancellation date for an invoice in VBRK?
entry.content:
I couldn’t find how to delete my own posts, but here’s what I’ve discovered:

The table VBRK has a column called SFAKN, which contains the number of the original canceled document. Additionally, in our specific implementation of SAP, the column VBRK-VBTYP holds the value ‘N’ for cancellation documents. Therefore, I used the following SQL statement:

 

 

  SELECT SINGLE fkdat FROM vbrk
    WHERE sfakn = @p_vbeln
      AND vbtyp = 'N'
    INTO _date.

 

 

Hope this helps.

entry.title: Re: User login take long time

Please, advice

entry.title: Re: Is SQL Anywhere viable and sustainable
entry.content:

This is copied from my product page:

 

Details for Product Version SAP SQL ANYWHERE 17.0
Official Name:SAP SQL Anywhere 17.0
Product:SYBASE SQL ANYWHERE
Product Category & Line:Technology Platform > SAP SQL Anywhere
Type of Product Version:Standalone
Release Type:Standard
Current Status:Unrestricted available 
SAP HANA Readiness: 
 

 

Ready  16.07.2015 Further Information
Release Plan
Release to Customer (RTC):16.07.2015
General Availability:16.07.2015
End of Mainstream Maintenance:31.12.2028

entry.title: Re: API-To retrieve ICOs from PO system
entry.content: @Madhu29 it takes two steps to get the details - 1st step is to query the list of keys, and the 2nd step is to invoke the service to read the details for each unique key. I don't have the specifics any longer because I don't have access to a PI/PO system.

entry.title: Communication error when sending with HTTP, Idoc to HTTP destination
entry.content:
Hi,

I am trying to send idoc to HTTP destination using background job. Idoc gets created but sending fails.

Communication error when sending with HTTP

The error code and the error message are: 411 /Connect to XXXXXX:443 failed: NIECONN_REFUSED(-10).

Same works fine when done manually, but not in job. Connection test is ok in SM59.

Cannot find anythig helpfull with error code 411...

Any ideas what might be the problem when sending in background?

Regards,

Pekka

entry.title: Re: How to get the cancellation date for an invoice in VBRK?
entry.content: To get an answer you should use a better tag such as SD (Sales and Distribution) or even ABAP Development

entry.title: Re: A Practical Guide to Naming iFlows in SAP Cloud Integration (SCI)
entry.content:
Hello,

how do you deal with Iflows that have several endpoints or are subprocesses that are called via direct process? Your example only display quite simple scenarios.

entry.title: Re: Mass processing of Business Partners using MDC in FIORI(Part-1)
entry.content:
Hi @Tahir_Aksoy ,

I see, I would suggest couple of things here.

1. Instead of using a file, please use the Mass processing for products app to select few materials manually and perform the update. This will eliminate any issues which might be there in the file format or the data.

2. Use file download functionality, Export products(F3251) to download a file, make your own changes and then upload again.

If it still doesn't work, then try to check as described in note 3098273 - Error - Changing worklist content failed in Fiori App F1047A.

Regards

Aniban

Regards

Anirban

Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Double slash (//) issue in Adobe forms
entry.content:
I have a text field in adobe forms layout. Value is filled dynamically from abap. When I fill data contains string "//+Character" then it considers this like a carriage return. But when I fill data contains string "//+Numeric" it is all good.

For Example

DATA 1:

 

//S/1231231231231231d.1231.231113,1231,31231231

 

Display:

//

S/1231231231231231d.1231.231113,1231,31231231

DATA 2: 

 

//1/1231231231231231d.1231.231113,1231,31231231

 

Display:

//1/1231231231231231d.1231.231113,1231,31231231

Why doest it go to new line after //S in DATA 1 ?

entry.title: Re: Communication error when sending with HTTP, Idoc to H...
entry.content:
So basically, connection from SAP GUI (manually) is OK, but from Application server (nackground job) is KO?

There should be a gateway issue or IP address not maintained correctly. Check for the IP address. Then update it in central instance, application server and firewall (Also white list)

Also use tools such as the ICM trace, via transaction SMICM.

entry.title: CAP LLM plugin vs. AI SDK
entry.content:
Hello there!

There are several SDKs for AI Core existing (for example here) and the CAP LLM plugin.

My question would be: when should I use which? Are there important differences in their application areas?

Thanks and best regards,

Leonie

entry.title: Re: How to manage Workflows for Sales Price in SAP S/4HANA ?
entry.content:

I have a question: If the business case requires approval for conditions with values over a specific amount, let's say 1000 EUR, how can we manage that scenario? In the step pre-conditions I only saw the condition type, sales org and distribution channel. Couldn't find any possibility to trigger workflow bases on the value of the condition.

Thank you in advance for the help!

Roberto

entry.title: Re: LO_BCS Creates folder in client's outlook for PDF print.

entry.title: Re: Double slash (//) issue in Adobe forms
entry.content: You'll need to escape the / character to get it to display how you wish.

entry.title: Where can I learn to set up the SAP Order Management for sourcing and availability solution?
entry.content:
I  recommend to access our Implementing SAP Order Management Sourcing and Availability  learning journey.

Overview
This course is designed to provide comprehensive knowledge and skills for implementing SAP Order Management Sourcing and Availability. You will explore the key concepts, analyze data ingestion methods, configure system settings, and understand the calculations behind product availability and sourcing for webshop orders. This course is designed to deliver critical insights into optimizing and effectively manage order sourcing and availability processes.

Learning objectives

After completing this course, you will be able to:

  1. Evaluate the principles of SAP Consumer Industry Cloud Architecture and Design.
  2. Explain the Sourcing Concept based on business goals.
  3. Analyze the essential elements of the SAP Order Management sourcing and availability reservation process.
  4. Review and apply data ingestion and replication frameworks.
  5. Configure SAP Order Management sourcing and availability functions.
  6. Calculate product availability and sourcing for webshop orders.

Prerequisites

  • Exploring SAP Order Management Foundation and Sourcing & Availability

Pass all the quizzes and receive a digital badge

Please ask a question related to the digital learning Journey in the Q&A area. 

Our SAP Learning Experts will get back to you as soon as possible! We are here to support you. 
I appreciate your feedback and we will make sure to continue sharing interesting topics.

Kind regards
Margit

entry.title: Re: A Practical Guide to Naming iFlows in SAP Cloud Integration (SCI)
entry.content:
Hi @Peter_555,

Multiple endpoints are usually not a problem, because all those endpoints should be part of the same partner - and iflow is for partner and process. 

Of course rule has always exceptions, so if you have specific integration flow, which belongs to more partners (for some reason) you can reflect that also in the name. eg. add multi or just prefix "m". I`ll leave it to your imagination. Just be consistent.

And for process direct - its also easy (imho), process direct is just another type of adapter, so you will use it in the name - or maybe its shorter form like PD or ProcDir

 

 

SCI00200150_IN_VeloCorp_HTTP_ProcDir_OrderRequest

 

Remark: Process Direct iflows are most likely used inside of chain of iflows - so they do not communicate with partner, but they are more like "internal" ones. So you cannot say, that they are IN or OUT. They are INT (like internal).

So it will become

SCI00200150_INT_VeloCorp_HTTP_ProcDir_OrderRequest

entry.title: Re: Enable Now Producer not playing
entry.content: Thank you ! removing the Live review script fixed it. that's why it was working in the main content WA and not the sandbox.

entry.title: Re: Enable Now Producer not playing
entry.content: Gah, you're right (of course!). I'd even seen that same issue at one of my clients, traced it to Live Review and removed it. But I have other clients where it is still working OK, so I put it down to my own incompatible 'customization' and not a wider problem. Either way, I'd appreciate you sending me the latest LR files, too. Thanks!

entry.title: Re: Compensation Route Map
entry.content:
As Xavier mentioned, we do not recommend using a 7 step route map. Leading Practice would be Comp Admin - Employee - Manager - Comp Admin. However, if you wish to add more steps you can add a custom role.

In your case since you're using the Second Manager hierarchy you'd want.

Employee (E)
Employee's Second Manager (EA)
Then from there you can do a custom role for EAA, EAAA, EAAAA, etc for how many approvals you need. For those where those steps do not apply for example a Director or VP won't have 7 levels above them, the system would skip those steps. Note that there is a maximum limit of 7 (EAAAAAAA) as noted in the KBA I posted below.

See common role codes here https://me.sap.com/notes/2087940

I highly recommend a Comp Admin step before and after the manager review steps as this allows the Comp team to look over worksheets, etc before they get to the managers and allows for corrections/relaunching worksheets at the start of the process if issues are found without impacting managers and losing planning they may have entered. Then having that step at the end allows you to control when worksheets go to completed.

entry.title: Re: SAP Hybris C4C digital signature in quote and order
entry.content:
@loganathan_p3

entry.title: Screen Personas SP18 transport from one system to another
entry.content:
Hello everyone,

I have a problem with SAP Screen Personas , and I don't know what to do. I have created a flavor for the transaction BP

(tab address, I have removed the address data here )

(tab Identification, here i have removed almost every field from the organization data)

 

However, if I want to transport this flavor via the /N/personas/admin transaction and also via the function export and import in the target system.

Some changes are taken over and others are not, I have already added all the necessary notes that occurred during the health check and also created a new flavor without scripting to see if it is possibly due to scripting, but the result is the same, in my target system.

(target system, all my changes have been implemented in the “Address” tab )

But in the identification tab my changes were not included

 

Do you have any experience with transports with screen personas that might help here?

Best regards Dominik

entry.title: Do you know the key features, capabilities, and concepts integral to SAP Order Management foundation
entry.content:
I  recommend to access our Implementing SAP Order Management Foundation learning journey.

Overview
This course offers an in-depth look into the implementation of SAP Order Management Foundation covering key features, capabilities, and essential components to successfully set up and utilize SAP Order Management Foundation.

Learning objectives
By the end of this course, you will be able to:

  • Analyze key features and capabilities of SAP Order Management Foundation.
  • Evaluate necessary components for successful setup.
  • Analyze the order submission process and stages of order orchestration.

Prerequisites

  • Exploring SAP Order Management Foundation and Sourcing & Availability

Pass all the quizzes and receive a digital badge

Please ask a question related to the digital learning Journey in the Q&A area. 

Our SAP Learning Experts will get back to you as soon as possible! We are here to support you. 
I appreciate your feedback and we will make sure to continue sharing interesting topics.

Kind regards
Margit

entry.title: Re: Easy way to create APIs in SAP using standard classes
entry.content:
Great blog! Thanks.

In order to avoid all the ASSIGN COMPONENT xyz commands in the IF_REST_RESOURCE~POST method, I'd propose the following approach:

  METHOD if_rest_resource~post.

    TYPES:
      BEGIN OF ty_data,
        name(50),
        city(50),
        region(20),
        pin(6),
      END OF ty_data.

    TYPES: tt_data TYPE STANDARD TABLE OF ty_data WITH DEFAULT KEY.

    TYPES:
      BEGIN OF ty_root,
        testapi TYPE tt_data,
      END OF ty_root.

    DATA: s_root TYPE ty_root.

    DATA(lv_request_body) = mo_request->get_entity( )->get_string_data( ).

    /ui2/cl_json=>deserialize(
      EXPORTING
        json             = lv_request_body
      CHANGING
        data             = s_root
       ).

ENDMETHOD.

entry.title: PhD Networking at SAP Labs Munich - The Power of Community and Research
entry.content:
On Wednesday, the 2nd of October, SAP-affiliated and collaborating PhD candidates came together at SAP Labs Munich. This event served for community building, networking, and professional exchange, fully embracing SAP Munich Labs’ philosophy of "Creating Impact Together". It brought together PhD candidates from various programs and institutions, such as the University Alliances PhD Graduates Program, the strategic partner UCC Munich, and joint SAP&TUM research projects.

The event centered on building a community and providing the opportunity to connect, learn from one another, and foster meaningful cross-institutional exchange. The gathering highlighted the value of maintaining research connections between PhD candidates and SAP and undermined the importance of industry-academia collaboration and the interface of research and application. As SAP continues to expand its academic partnerships and support for PhD candidates, we are excited to see the future innovations that will emerge from these efforts.

Dr. Katharina Schäfer, Global Head of University Alliances (UA), kicked off the event by emphasizing the value of strong networks, drawing from her own experience as a former PhD. Just this year, UA launched its PhD Program and onboarded the first PhDs. Also, Dr. Rüdiger Eichin and Dr. Tobias Müller from the SAP&TUM Industry-University Collaboration team gave an engaging presentation on the SAP&TUM approach, focusing on Munich’s ecosystem as the perfect hub for research-driven product innovation.

Besides a tour of the new SAP Labs Campus Munich which showcases the interdisciplinary, synergetic work between SAP and the TUM departments, participants spent time to network and present their research to each other - leading to multiple cross-institutional project ideas. In further group discussions, they brainstormed ideas on staying connected and supporting one another and future PhDs both professionally and personally. The event was just the kick-off to many more.

After the formal program, the PhDs were transferred to the Oktoberfest to continue the discussions, socialize in a casual atmosphere and enjoy the German traditions.

 

Further Links

SAP Research Community 

CRS Central Research Services 

UCC TUM

SAP Labs Munich and TUM Campus

entry.title: Retrieve assigned IAS Groups in SAPUI5 Application (Cloud Foundry)
entry.content:
Hello SAP Experts,

I’m facing an issue with retrieving user properties, specifically groups, in my SAPUI5 application, which is deployed in a Cloud Foundry environment with an on-premise ABAP backend.

I had been using the User API Service (https://help.sap.com/docs/btp/sap-business-technology-platform/user-api-service) to fetch user information, including groups, and populating a JSON model for UI validation (e.g., showing/hiding buttons based on user roles). Until last week, this was working fine, but now I’m encountering an issue where the groups property is coming back as undefined when I debug the data.

Here's the code that I'm using to fetch the data from User Api:

 

const oUserData = this.getView().getModel("modelUser").getData();
this.setModel(new JSONModel(this.validateUserActions(oUserData.groups)), "rolModel");

 

What could be the reason for this issue, and are there any alternative methods to achieve the same functionality in my scenario (Cloud Foundry SAPUI5 Frontend / On-Premise ABAP Backend)?

If retrieving groups from IAS is not possible anymore, is there a way to fetch Role Collections directly from SAP BTP Cockpit or any other method to retrieve user role/authorization data in this setup?

I would greatly appreciate any insights or suggestions on how to resolve this issue. Thank you in advance for your time and assistance!
Looking forward to your feedback,

Cristian

entry.title: Re: How to uncheck down payments to draw into credit memo using API and/or Service Layer?
entry.content: Does anyone has an update on this? We have the same issue and need a workaround on this. Some customers required creating the target document without having the downpayment applied.

entry.title: PO Accounting Split Value by Amount Error
entry.content:
Hello,

Running into an issue when creating a PO with accounting split by Amount (over 10 splits) and when invoice is ready to be processed manually in Ariba for the amount less than the PO amount, we receive an error that Line Item 1, Accounting: The Split values amount is more than total amount.

But when creating a PO with accounting split by Percentage (over 10 splits), invoice is manually processed successful without any errors. But when invoices are processed via ICS Paper Invoice method, both PO scenarios by amount or percentage accounting splits are successfully processed and integrated to Ariba.

entry.title: Re: API Testing in SAP Build Actions Dropping Part of Request
entry.content:
Thank you for reaching out to us. Could you kindly cross-check the following configuration?

1. Verify Field Mappings in SAP Build Actions
The "FieldValueMap" may not be correctly mapped in the Actions tab. Ensure that:

  • The field name in the API request exactly matches the expected field in the API schema.
  • The field is configured correctly in the input payload of the Actions setup.

2. Check for Default Payload Handling
The SAP Build Actions tab might sanitize or omit fields if they are not properly configured as part of the request payload.

  • Review the payload configuration in the Actions tab and ensure that the "FieldValueMap" field is explicitly included.
  • If the field is optional in the API schema, check if Build is unintentionally dropping it due to its nature.

entry.title: MDK Code Not Working on Android Device - Unable to Redraw Sectioned Table
entry.content:
Hello SAP Community,

I'm currently working on an MDK (Mobile Development Kit) application and facing an issue with redrawing a sectioned table on an Android device. The code snippet I'm using is as follows:

let container2 = context.getPageProxy().getControl('SectionedTable0');
container2.redraw();

This code works perfectly on other platforms, but it doesn't seem to have any effect on my Android device. I've checked for errors in the console and ensured that all necessary permissions are set. Despite this, the section does not redraw as expected.

Here are some additional details:

  • MDK Version: 24.11.0
  • Android Version: 11

Steps I've taken so far:

  1. Verified the code works on other platforms.
  2. Checked for any console errors.
  3. Ensured all necessary permissions are granted.

Has anyone else encountered a similar issue or have any suggestions on how to resolve this? Any help would be greatly appreciated!

Thank you in advance!

entry.title: Re: Double slash (//) issue in Adobe forms
entry.content: For example replacing "/" Forward slash with "\/" - Escaped characters

entry.title: Re: SAP Build Process Automation: Writing formatted text...
entry.content:
Hello Adam, 

Thanks for reaching out to us, here are listed SDK supported by our SAP Build Process Automation.  https://help.sap.com/docs/intelligent-robotic-process-automation/cloud-studio-user-guide/sdk-packages?locale=en-US&q=sdk

entry.title: More than 4 consumers for a Single source system in SLT
entry.content:
Hi Team,

 

  1. Is it possible to create more than 4 1:N configurations irrespective of the SLTs systems for a single source.
  2. Is it possible to create more than 4 1:1 Configurations with DMIS 2011 SP17 SLTs and source.

 

We want to create a 5th 1:N configuration for the same source.


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: More than 4 consumers for a Single source system in SLT
entry.content:
Dear Customer,

 

Please see KBA 2796027 - How many times can a table be replicated using SLT?

Please note for DMIS 2011 SP17, the limitation is on the table level, so if you want to replicate a table more than 4 times you need to follow the below steps and not use "allow multiple usage" in the 5th configuration. If you are still only replicating tables a maximum of 4 times then you do not need to do the below steps. 

  • Enabling multiple 1:1 configurations is possible by performing the following steps:
    1. Go to transaction SE16
    2. Enter the table IUUC_RT_PARAMS
    3. Click 'Create' 
    4. Click 'New Entries' 
    5. Add the parameter 'MULTI_1_1" and set its value to X
    6. Press 'Save' to save changes 

 

Best Regards,

SAP Support

entry.title: Re: Not able to purchase one/six attempt for SAP certific...
entry.content: Every India people interested by CER001 or CER006 has to create a case?

entry.title: Re: Using APIs to Migrate Incentive Management Commission...
entry.content: Hi Ron - We use the API for migrations. I recommend using the pipeline endpoint with the XMLImport command. For the objects that do not get exported to XML e.g. titles and classifiers we use those respective endpoints. Feel free to reach out if you want more information or a demonstration.

entry.title: Re: Is SQL Anywhere viable and sustainable
entry.content:
You can find this information in

  • PAM Product Availability Matrix Details for Product Version SAP SQL ANYWHERE 17.0 'Unrestricted available' but until end of 2028
  • OSS KBA 2381119 - About the latest version of SQL Anywhere SAP Knowledge Base Article 
  • Online help SAP SQL Anywhere Supported Platforms and Engineering Support Status

entry.title: Re: "Runtime error - Description of Exception". When post...
entry.content:
The error indicates that it's trying to make an RFC call to GTS, but the destination in question does not exist.

 

Regards,

Ryan Crosby

entry.title: Re: Passing Bas64 Encoded string of a PDF as Email attachment from Process Automation
entry.content:
Hello Jibin, Thanks for reaching out to us, 

1. Direct Usage of Base64 Content for Email Attachment

Most email services or APIs (including the ones integrated into SAP BPA) support attachments in Base64 format. You can directly use the Base64 string in your email task or workflow.

  • Steps:

    1. Ensure the Base64 string you fetch from AWS S3 is valid and includes the MIME type.
    2. In the BPA email action, add the Base64 string as an attachment.
    3. Specify the MIME type (application/pdf) and filename explicitly.

  • Example Configuration for Email Task:

    • Attachment Data: The Base64 string from S3.
    • Attachment Name: The filename (e.g., document.pdf).
    • Attachment MIME Type: application/pdf.

This avoids storing the file locally or using DMS, as the Base64 content is passed directly.

entry.title: Date prefilled as Yesterday in Calender
entry.content:
Hi All,

I am looking out for a calender to be appearing on the top of the SAC story with yesterday's date selected and if today is Monday then it should have selected last friday. Is there any way we can acheieve it in story filter.

Regards,

Singh

entry.title: Re: Datasphere Analytic Model oData: 404 Resource Not Found
entry.content: Hmm, while calling the API you would try Developer Tools (Ctrl+Shift+I) in your browser and monitor Console and Network, etc. It may give you some clue for the cause!

entry.title: How to DISABLE the Presentation Mode in SAC?
entry.content:
Hello,

Does anyone know how to DISABLE the new functionality - presentation mode in SAP SAC? It happens to be confusing for our users and the comments don't get saved when they enter in that mode. So we want to disable it. It's useless for us.

SAP Analytics Cloud, hybrid analytics

entry.title: Re: Populate "Created by" and "Created on" in the Input r...
entry.content: Hello Surbhi, Thank you for reaching out to us. Have you had a chance to review the solution proposed by Gregor? If you need further clarification or have additional details to share, please let us know so we can investigate further.

entry.title: Value Driver Tree drill down - SAC design
entry.content:
Hello, 

I've created a simple VDT in an optimized story.

 

Drill down to customers/sku works well.

 

However, every attempt to drill up or collapse fails, and I lose the first aggregated design - I'm always getting back to the customers I've selected.

example no1 - collapse from the revenue will just hide/reveal the customers I've selected

example no2: trying to collapse the European customers - it collapses only the European rebates

 

 

 

 

 

 

When is this bug expected to be corrected?

Deb

entry.title: Re: SAP Build Process Automation Subcription Failed
entry.content:
Hi Sayanasatyarpa,

Thank you for reaching out to us.

On the error screen, if you click on "More Details," you should see the reason for the failure. In the meantime, please ensure that the entitlement is enabled and all prerequisites for SAP BPA are met.

Feel free to update us if you continue to face any issues.

entry.title: Delivery Output Determination in shipment document missing
entry.content:
Hi all,

I have an issue regards the output determination in the shipment document.

Pratically, When I create the shipment document the system automatically link the output message type to the shipment. The problem is that when I link the delivery document to the shipment, the output message type of the Ship-to automatically disappear.

Can anyone help me? Maybe I miss something or I don't know.

Thanks

Bye

entry.title: Re: /SCWM/RWEIGHT_VOLUME_UPDATE in Public Edition
entry.content: Hi Clark, You are correct. We found the same root cause. It appears we loaded our bins before the warehouse attributes were transported to the Test system. Our Productive system has not yet been issued and will ensure the correct sequence is followed for this load. If the correction report does not work, we can the re-load data. Thank you for your time.

entry.title: Re: Enable Now Producer Functionalities
entry.content:
Hello Romina, most of your questions are answered in the SAP Enable Now Info Center website at https://enable-now.sap.com . It's free to sign up with your email or SAP ID. 

  • Are there standard templates available in the producer or does the customer needs to create custom templates for E-Learnings? - There are a few standard Book Styles with clickable buttons available in the Resources of your workarea. You can also build your own Book Style. You can designate any Book Page (slide) as a Book Page Template in its Properties, and control the appearance of your Book Pages with the Book Page Template. You can download more Book Styles and Book Page Templates from the SAP Enable Now Info Center. Most of my customers build their own. 
  • Is there any example scorm content generated with the producer to show to the customer and test the integration with their LMS? - No, you need to build a Book (a single Sharable Content Object/SCO), with a couple of Book Pages, a Simulation with all the playback modes you intend to use, and maybe a Quiz. Then publish it as whatever flavor of SCORM you need, and test it in your LMS. The results are highly variable depending on your content, your SCORM version, and your LMS. You can also go to SCORM.com (run by Rustici Software), and download a sample SCORM package for testing. You can also test your own SCORM packages there and get automated feedback on what's wrong with your package. 
  • Are there functions like interacting persons who can be inserted in the book pages? - if you mean Avatars, yes, the Comic Man and Comic Woman (editable avatars) are in your Resources. You can download others, like Dotty, or a series of non-editable avatars, from the SAP Enable Now Info Center website. Most of my customers don't use avatars.

You can purchase SAP Enable Now implementation assistance through SAP or its SEN-certified partners. Contact your SAP salesperson for information. The SEN implementation consultant will help you set up your SEN, configure it for what you want to do, and guide you in the proper use and care of SEN.

entry.title: Datasphere Task Chain Mail Notifications are not working
entry.content:
Herkese merhaba

Aşağıdaki gibi alt görev zincirlerini içeren bir ana görev zinciri oluşturdum: 

İşlem başarıyla veya başarısızlıkla tamamlandığında bana bildirimde bulunması için hem alt zincirler hem de ana zincir için e-posta bildirimleri ayarladım. Ancak, başarılı veya başarısız olduğunda e-posta göndermiyor.


Bir Görev Zinciri başarısız bir şekilde tamamlandığında ve e-posta gönderilemediğinde oluşan hatayla ilgili bir SAP Notu buldum:  SAP Notu 0003440103  Bu Not, başarısız görev zinciri görevleri için Datasphere'de bir hata olduğunu belirtir.
Başarılı Görev Zinciri görevleri için de benzer bir durum var mı, yoksa eksik bir e-posta yapılandırması mı var yoksa başka bir sorun mu var?
Aylar önce görev zincirini ilk denediğimde aldığım e-posta bildiriminin ekran görüntüsünü gönderiyorum.

Bilgisi olan varsa bilgi verirse sevinirim.

entry.title: Re: SAP Build App - Multiple Dates Field alignment
entry.content:
Hi Anam, 

the issue of date fields not being fully visible despite resolving the overlapping with Z-index could be due to constraints in the container size or padding/margin settings. Here are a few suggestions to address this:

  1. Adjust Container Size:
    Ensure the container holding the date fields is large enough to accommodate the full content of the fields. You can do this by increasing the width and height of the container.

  2. Padding and Margin Settings:
    Review the padding and margin settings of the container and the date fields. Sometimes, insufficient spacing can cause clipping. Add extra padding or margin to ensure the fields have enough room to display fully.

  3. Field Alignment:
    Verify the alignment settings for the date fields. Set them to "Center" or "Stretch" as per your layout requirements to ensure they occupy the available space properly.

  4. Scaling or Font Adjustment:
    Check the font size and scaling of the date fields. Reducing the font size slightly might help fit the content without needing large gaps.

  5. Responsive Layout:
    If the app is responsive, test it on different screen sizes. Adjust the layout settings (e.g., flex or grid layout) to ensure the date fields are visible across various devices.

  6. Inspect Element Styles:
    Use the debugging tools (like the developer console in the browser) to inspect the styles applied to the fields. Look for overflow, clip, or hidden properties that might be causing the issue and adjust them.

If the issue persists after applying these suggestions, please provide additional details, such as screenshots or the layout setup, for further assistance.

entry.title: Re: Vendor Payment Process After Item Is Sold
entry.content:
Hi

follow-up questions.

1. For the retail industry with multiple stores, how can consignment stock be effectively managed across various locations?

It can be managed as per sites and stores, so you can transfer stock to respective sites or stores. 

2. If a customer returns an item after the invoice has been submitted, how can it be differentiated within the consignment process?

Make a agreement to payment to vendor with time line to customer problem so you will get a gap to see whether it is a customer returns or not ( but it is a challenge to deal)

3. Regarding sales quantities, should we rely on reports, or is there a way to integrate this directly into the consignment process?

As per my knowledge, It is better to transfer to own premises using 411 and then need to sell it

4. Are there any best practices for managing consignment settlement and tracking in a retail environment?

Really That you need to explore. I have not checked it . I am sorry to it 

 

Regards

Rashid

entry.content:

I know that ASE 16.1 is being released Q1 2025 and SP04 PL07 is being released Q2 2025, but will there be any more patch releases for 16.0 after SP04 PL07?

Thanks in advance

entry.title: Re: SAP Signavio Language of Folders
entry.content: Hi @Stephen_Niemann, as you were referring to the folder names and diagram titles, I am afraid that Kristof's answer (from the Support Team) is the valid one. I made the false assumption that the same rules apply here as for the language settings for the menu/labels in the application, but unfortunately this is not the case. Sorry for the confusion.

entry.title: Re: Talent Intelligence Hub - Myths and Misconceptions - Part 1 (Prerequisites)
entry.content: @Avinash9 Have you run the Check Tool in all areas connected to the Job Description Manager and Job Profile Builder (including any associated mappings)? If any errors result, they need to be resolved prior to attempting the migration/upgrade to TIH, otherwise it will fail.

entry.title: Do you know the key components, functionalities, and real-world applications of SAP Order Management
entry.content:
I  recommend to access our Exploring SAP Order Management Foundation and Sourcing & Availability learning journey.

Overview
This course provides comprehensive insights into the key components, functionalities, and real-world applications of SAP Order Management. It covers foundational concepts, omni-channel fulfillment challenges, and customer-centric use cases to enhance your skills in SAP Order Management solutions.

Learning objectives
By the end of this course, you will be able to:

  • Identify and address key challenges in omni-channel order management.
  • Understand and utilize the core functionalities of SAP Order Management Foundation.
  • Apply order orchestration concepts and manage order activities & events.
  • Integrate analytics to gain business insights.
  • Explore functionalities for sourcing and availability management.
  • Analyze and implement best practices from various customer use cases.

Pass all the quizzes and receive a digital badge

Please ask a question related to the digital learning Journey in the Q&A area. 

Our SAP Learning Experts will get back to you as soon as possible! We are here to support you. 
I appreciate your feedback and we will make sure to continue sharing interesting topics.

Kind regards
Margit

entry.title: Recurring Process and Multi-Selection of Activities in Process Scheduler
entry.content:
Hello SAP Community!  

Today marks the last blogpost for the SAP Profitability and Performance Management (SAP PaPM) Cloud – Standard Model for the year 2024.  

As we have introduced the Process Scheduler during Q3 of 2022 in this blogpost, Process Scheduler: Execution made easy by Scheduling a “Job” in SAP Profitability and Performance Management Cloud, we are pleased to announce the new enhancements of the Process Scheduler.  

Previously, a scheduled job in Process Scheduler could run a specific activity in a process template or process instance at a specific date and time. But great news everyone! Due to the requests of our customers, I am delighted to announce that our Process Scheduler can now handle recurring processes to which you can schedule a job that will run activities every day, every hour, every month, and so on. Yes! You’ve read it right. A job can now handle multiple activities in a process template or process instance that will run sequentially. 

How will it work? Let’s dive right in.  

In adding a new Job or copying through Job From, there are 2 new fields in “Add process schedule” dialogue, namely Repeat Interval and End time.  

1. Repeat Interval – In this field, you can specify the interval for the job to repeat. You can enter a number followed by a time unit such as 2 days, 1 week, 3 months, etc. For instance, if you enter “2 days”, the job will be triggered every 2 days at the scheduled launch time. Only numbers and English time units (for example, days, weeks, months) are currently being accepted.  

Note: This feature uses the SAP Job Scheduling Service. For detailed instructions on entering the repeat interval correctly, see Human-Readable Date Formats section in SAP Job Scheduling Service Cloud documentation.  

2. End time – This field will be enabled once “Repeat Interval” is maintained. In this section, you can set an end date and time to when the recurring runs will stop. If this is left blank, it will also be accepted and will run recurrently that will only stop by deleting the job created.  

3. Activities - This field is where we can select 1 or more Execution Activities in a process template or process instance that we would choose to run sequentially based on the order of selection made.  

After confirmation, the information about the job created will be displayed on top of the list in the left-hand side panel. Let’s highlight the column header “Next planned launch”, which was previously named “Planned launch”, that will show when the next run recurrence is happening. In our case, this is the first run. Also, there is a new column heading introduced here, which is the “Repeat Interval”.  

Now let’s focus on the right-hand side panel.  

Validation of the execution status of the recurring processes can be checked under “Runs” located in the right-hand side panel.  

Runs – Displays information about each job run. You can see the sequence number of the currently displayed job run in relation to a total number of scheduled job runs (for example 1/2 or 2/2), and you can switch between them using the arrows next to the job count. 

Now, I will show you an example of how it works. In this example, we will schedule a job that will run 2 activities every 5 minutes with a total of 4 Runs.  

After confirming, the system will create a job, which will appear at the top of the list of scheduled jobs with the status “SCHEDULED”. The panel on the right will show more detailed information about the job run.  

After the 1st successful run, the next planned launch will be changed to the next scheduled recurrence and the state will still be in “SCHEDULED” status. The Runs will create another page below the scheduled Job run page (2/2). 

After the 2nd successful run, the next planned launch will be changed to the next scheduled recurrence and the state will still be in “SCHEDULED” status. The Runs will create another page below the scheduled job run 2/3.  

After the 3rd successful run, the next planned launch will be changed to the next scheduled recurrence and the state will still be in “SCHEDULED” status. The Runs will create another page below the scheduled job run.   

After the 4th successful run, since it will be the last recurrence, the state of the job will now be set to “COMPLETED” and all the runs are now set to ”COMPLETED”. 

And there you have it. Like and share this blog post with your colleagues who may be interested in this new feature. Watch out for future enhancements and improvements of the Process Scheduler. This information is also available in our Application Help

Thank you and Happy holidays!

entry.title: Re: Build and Run Multi-Tenant SaaS Solutions on the SAP BTP
entry.content:
Hi Knut,

I am trying to run a few examples of CAP applications, but I am messed in many new terms.

What is difference between multi-tenant, multi-target and multi-backend applications/solutions? Is it the same thing or something else? Is it multi-target format outdated?

I managed to develop a few simple Fiori apps on base List Report Page template with header/items and actions. At least there is a good tutorial ("Build a Business Application Using CAP for Node.js") how to develop and deploy so-called "multi-target" CAP apps. It works anyway. Nevertheless I am stuck with getting access to my data from an external app (from Postman, for example). The Poetry Slams (partner-reference-application) tutorial has a frightening size and contents a lot of redundant things for us. I tried to perform all steps of "42a Enable API Access to SAP BTP Applications Using Service Broker" topic but the broker service failed with crash during the deployment. Another tutorial "Secure a Basic Node.js App with the Authorization and Trust Management Service (XSUAA)" does not work either - it is not entirely clear how to just run the "Product List" app in Business Application Studio. At least the "cds watch" way does not work. I would wish to see something more like a "hello, world" example. Could you please recommend some simpler tutorial on authorization and OData API, probably without the multi-tenant feature?

Thank you.

Aleksei Belousov

entry.title: Re: Number Range for Serial Number Profile
entry.content: How to connect serial number range with serial number profile so that it will be created during production order release?

entry.title: Re: "Runtime error - Description of Exception". When posting PGI from VL02N
entry.content:
Note carefully 'No RFC destination is defined for SAP Global Trade Services' (missing configuration in this system?)

  • Read KBA 2127646 - KBA: No RFC destination is defined for SAP Global Trade Services Message no. /SAPSLL/PLUGINR3002
  • If GTS actually required, read 2447201 - KBA: Where to find GTS Documentation

entry.title: SAP Community Call for ALM
entry.content:
The free monthly webinar provides everything you need to know about SAP's Application Lifecycle Management (ALM) offerings. Get the latest news directly from SAP's topic experts!

With the monthly SAP Community Call for ALM, you have the chance to stay updated about what's going on with SAP Cloud ALM and SAP Focused Run.

For slides and recordings click here.

Please register here!

entry.title: Re: Approver workflow for standard fiori application
entry.content:
Hello Vijay, Thanks for reaching out to us, 

es, it is technically possible to integrate a business approval workflow into a standard Fiori application like the "Manage Cost Centers (Version 2)" (F1443A) app while adhering to a clean core approach. Here’s how you can achieve this:

1. Extend the Standard Fiori App Using Adaptation

SAP provides tools like UI Adaptation and In-App Extensions in SAP S/4HANA, which allow adding custom fields or logic without modifying the core application. Use these tools to introduce a mechanism where the creation process triggers a workflow.

2. Business Workflow Integration

  • Workflow Trigger: Implement a pre-check mechanism that triggers the workflow when a new cost center is about to be created. This can be done using BAdIs (Business Add-Ins) or Event-Driven Architecture (e.g., CAP or RAP-based events) to invoke the workflow.

  • Approval Workflow: Create an approval workflow in SAP Build Process Automation 

    • Input: Pass the requestor information and app ID (F1443A) as part of the workflow payload.
    • Logic: Use SAP Business Rules service to determine the approver based on the input data (e.g., app ID and requestor).
    • Approval Task: Route the approval task to the SAP My Inbox app of the approver.

entry.title: Create attachment (photo) with personas on SE on mobile doesn't work
entry.content:
Hello,

I've created a flavor to create attachment on purchase requisition via the camera.

It's working fine on pc on windows with a browser.

On Android mobile, first I get this error (see image), that I don't get on pc.

When I continue, I can open the camera, then on firefox when trying to confirm the upload, I get a dump: "Exception condition "X_ERROR" triggered".

If I switch to Chrome browser, I don't get the dump but same error message.

Thanks,

Emmanuel.

entry.title: Re: SAP Content Server 7.53 Installation and Connectivity with ERP using SSL
entry.content:
Hello, SAP do not recommend to use "/sapcs" but "sapcs" only for HTTP Script field.

When the URL is generated it will compose it based on the data of OAC0 Content Repository.
So this is basically telling the runtime to user http://myhost:1090//sapcs.... there will be an additional "/" character with the given configuration in your Blog.

Furthermore, on the CSADMIN settings.
You are using on section "5. Post-Configurations:" on the 3rd screenshot, you are using Database parameters mixed up with FileSystem storage type. This is incorrect.

The correct is to use "StorageDriver" for FSStorage and no further DB parameter references.

StorageDriver FSStorage
ContRepRoot X:\usr\sap\SCS
Security 1
ContRepDescription SAP CS New installation

 

Parameters are described on this SAP Note.
329473 - Description of Content Server and Cache Server configuration file

entry.title: Year-end closing for non-leading ledger in ECC
entry.content:
This is about the Balance carry forward customer/vendor (F.07) for non-leading ledger .

The Fiscal year for non-leading ledger is from Jan to Dec and FY for leading ledger is April to March. My question is how to perform the balance carry forward process for vendor/customer (F.07) for non-leading ledger in Dec to get the report correctly for Local reporting since this process is not ledger specific. 

Once the next fiscal year (say 2025) is opened for non-leading ledger, will the values of leading ledger reflect automatically in non-leading or how...?

Thanks in advance.

Balam

entry.title: Re: Templates on Disclosure Management
entry.content: Great work Rodrigo! Useful topic.

entry.title: Re: difference in fs10n and faglb03
entry.content: Hello,

entry.title: Re: difference in fs10n and faglb03
entry.content: Hello, could you please let me know, whether FS10N is selecting from GLTo and FAGLB03 is selecting from FAGLFLEXT? Regards, Wolfgang

entry.title: Re: Long time to run a report in VS2022 the first time
entry.content:
What SP are you using? What changed in the past 3 weeks? look for Windows patch history, has been known to cause problems.

SP 37 was just released:

https://help.sap.com/docs/SUPPORT_CONTENT/crystalreports/3354091173.html

See my Blog on upgrading:

https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blogs-by-sap/upgrading-a-visual-studio-2008-gt-2022-net-project-with-the-latest-cr-net/ba-p/13469215

I don't know why but it is normal for the first report to take more time due to the app loading all of the CR runtime.

It can also take time if you have any Verify DB options enabled in the Report Options, uncheck if not required.

Often it is recommended to load a dummy report when the app first starts up to preload the CR runtime.

entry.title: Re: Remove the share button from my SAP Fiori Element app...

entry.title: Issue with merging SFCs in SAP Digital Manufacturing
entry.content:
Hi DM Experts,

I used the SFC Split function to split a SFC into two SFCs, both linked to the same order. Following this, I attempted to merge the two SFCs back together using the SFC Merge function, but I received the following error message:

"Merge across operations is not possible on the standard routing."

 

 

Scenario Details

  • Split Operation: The original SFC was successfully split into two SFC

  • Routing Plan: Both SFCs are linked to a order-specific routing 

  • SFC Status: Both SFCs are currently in the "in the Queue" status

  • Operation: Both SFCs are assigned to the same operation at the same resource

Thank you in advance for your support!

Best regards,
Gizem

entry.title: Is there any way to restrict access of T-codes involved in FIORI standard catalogs for VA01 for SOD
entry.content:
Is it possible to restrict access to specific transactions within a standard catalog? Currently, we need to limit access to the VA01 transaction code in the standard catalog SAP_SD_BC_SO_PROC_OP, which contains multiple transactions such as VA01, VA02, and VA03.

I attempted to create a new catalog to achieve this, but I couldn't maintain groups in the new catalog. The system is connected to ECC and Fiori.

Could you guide me on whether there is a way to restrict access to only VA01 in ECC or through another approach?

entry.title: Re: Mismatch in CJI5/Table Commitment Values & PR value
entry.content: Run transaction CJBN to rebuild budget availability.  You can also run CJEN to rebuild the PS Info structure.  It's a good idea to schedule these transactions to run periodically.

entry.title: Companion links appearing in multiple apps.
entry.content:
We built SAP Companion links within one app, but they are appearing on multiple apps within Fiori. We tried deleting and re-adding the link, but the problem persists. We verified our Manager settings and everything looks correct. 

 

  • SAP FIORI FOR SAP S/4HANA 2023
  • SEN Cloud Edition 10.8.9 (build 5711)

entry.title: Hotkey Macro for SAP Applications
entry.content:
Hello everyone,

I’m looking for software recommendations to help automate some repetitive tasks in my workflow. Specifically, I need a tool that can:

  • Control programs through automatic text inputs or mouse clicks.
  • Book predefined materials to a project.
  • Book work activities (A, B, or C) of an employee (variable employee X, Y, or Z) to projects.

Since the materials, employees, and activities are always the same, I would like to store these in a table as variables and retrieve them with a hotkey.

In Past we used AutoHotkey for this Szenario. But due to some policy issues we are looking for an alternative Software. Is there an SAP product or external?

Any suggestions for software that can handle these requirements would be greatly appreciated!

Thank you in advance!

entry.title: Re: Error when modifying SY-SUBRC in S/4HANA Cloud Public...
entry.content: Hi @kaidehman, Thanks for your response. This is what I was looking for. Regards.
